title: Number of coral bleaching events by stage of the ENSO cycle
subtitle: [Coral bleaching](#dod:coral_bleaching) typically occurs when water temperatures rise above the normal range for the coral's habitat.  This is more likely during El Niño stages of the [ENSO cycle](#dod:enso_cycle) when tropical sea temperatures are warmer.
caption:
Number of coral bleaching events by stage of the ENSO cycle
Coral bleaching typically occurs when water temperatures rise above the normal range for the coral's habitat. This is more likely during El Niño stages of the ENSO cycle when tropical sea temperatures are warmer.

The bar chart displays the annual count of coral bleaching events from 1980 to 2016, categorized by the phase of the El Niño-Southern Oscillation (ENSO) cycle. The vertical axis measures the number of events, ranging from 0 to 80, while the horizontal axis tracks the years. Three distinct colors represent the ENSO stages: dark red for El Niño, purple for Moderate, and blue for La Niña. The data shows a fluctuating pattern where the frequency of bleaching events varies significantly depending on the year and the prevailing climate stage. The highest single-year count appears in 1998, where the El Niño bar reaches approximately 73 events. Another substantial peak occurs in 2016, with the El Niño bar rising to roughly 54 events. In contrast, the year 2010 shows the highest activity for the La Niña stage, with the blue bar reaching just over 50 events. Moderate stages show their highest activity in 2005, with the purple bar reaching 40 events. Other years, such as 1987 and 1982, show El Niño bars reaching roughly 26 and 22 events respectively. The years between 1990 and 1995 show lower counts, generally staying below 20 events across all categories. The distribution of bar heights indicates that while bleaching events occur during all stages, the largest annual totals coincide with El Niño years, followed by significant totals during La Niña years in specific instances like 2010. The chart shows that the number of recorded events generally increases in magnitude during the later years of the timeline compared to the early 1980s.
